key_points: The chapter focuses on the importance of persistence and the value of hard work.
It teaches that failure is just a stepping stone toward success and shouldn't be feared.
The central message is to keep trying until you reach your goal, no matter how many times you fall.
It encourages a positive 'can-do' attitude and building self-confidence.
Success is shown to be the result of effort, not just luck or talent.
examples: A student practicing a difficult math problem until they finally understand it.
An athlete training every day to improve their speed, even when they are tired.
The famous saying: 'Try, try, try again until you succeed.'
The story of the ant or the spider that keeps climbing even after falling down several times.
Completing a project that seemed impossible at first by taking small, steady steps.
formulas_or_processes: The Success Formula → Goal + Hard Work + Persistence = Success.
Handling Failure → Mistake → Learn from it → Try again with a better plan.
Progress → Doing a little bit more today than you did yesterday.
Mindset → Replacing 'I can't' with 'I will try my best'.
exam_tips: Remember the main moral: Never give up on your dreams.
Identify the difference between 'Giving up' and 'Trying hard'.
Be ready to explain why the journey is just as important as the final result.
